title: Libanotis iliensis
intro: Libanotis iliensis, also known as Ferula iliensis, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Libanotis iliensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
